skill.name: zoom-automation
skill.description: Automate Zoom meetings, webinars, recordings, participant reports, and usage workflows through Rube MCP and Composio Zoom tools.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents through authenticated Zoom automation using Rube MCP tool sequences.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static backtick, weak crypto, and reconnaissance detections are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and Zoom tool names in SKILL.md. The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated: the skill instructs agents to use a third-party Rube MCP endpoint for authenticated Zoom actions involving meetings, recordings, participant data, and deletion workflows.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Network Access for Zoom OAuth
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to add https://rube.app/mcp and authenticate a Zoom connection through Rube MCP. This is an intended integration, but it sends Zoom operations through a third-party service and requires user consent and account-scoped authorization.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded Rube MCP endpoint and OAuth connection workflow are explicit. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it creates a real third-party network and authorization dependency.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Zoom Recording and Download Token Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill includes workflows to list recordings, retrieve meeting recordings, request download access tokens, and use OAuth authorization for protected recordings. Recording files and tokens can expose sensitive meeting content if handled without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recording retrieval and download token parameters are directly documented. No exfiltration is shown, but the data type is sensitive and requires careful handling.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Zoom Recording Deletion Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill documents deletion of Zoom meeting recordings, including a permanent delete action. This is a legitimate administrative capability, but it can destroy meeting records if used without explicit user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The delete recording tool and permanent delete option are explicitly listed. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the skill describes normal Zoom administration.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick executions, but SKILL.md is Markdown and uses backticks for tool names and parameter names. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command substitution is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is Markdown inline code and table content, not executable source. The file contains instructions only, so command execution was not confirmed.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography on lines that contain the skill description and Zoom meeting listing guidance. No MD5, SHA1, encryption, hashing, or cryptographic implementation appears at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic code or algorithm names in context. This is a pattern-matching error against normal prose and tool naming.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Zoom API Context
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged reconnaissance-like terms, but the cited lines describe Zoom plan requirements, participant pagination, AI summary requirements, and rate limits. No host, network, or environment reconnaissance instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited context is Zoom account and API behavior documentation. It does not instruct the agent to inspect the local system or collect machine information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked Third-Party Tool Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires connecting to Rube MCP and using Zoom OAuth-backed tools. This pattern is expected for the capability, but it should be disclosed because meeting and account data may pass through a third-party integration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The MCP URL and connection workflow are explicitly documented. The risk is inherent to the architecture rather than hidden behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Administrative Delete Operation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a Zoom recording deletion tool with a permanent delete option. Agents should ask for explicit confirmation before using this workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The destructive action is clearly listed in the recording workflow and parameter guidance. It is legitimate but security-relevant.

content.user_title: Automate Zoom Workflows With Rube MCP
content.value_statement: Managing Zoom meetings, recordings, webinars, and reports takes repeated API steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Rube MCP workflows for common Zoom tasks.
actual_capabilities: Create scheduled, instant, and recurring Zoom meetings with key settings.
List, retrieve, update, and paginate through Zoom meeting records.
Find cloud recordings and meeting recording details within Zoom date limits.
Retrieve past meeting participants, usage reports, and AI meeting summaries.
List webinars and register participants when the account supports webinars.
Document Zoom-specific parameters, rate limits, encoding rules, and plan requirements.
limitations: Requires a working Rube MCP connection and an active Zoom OAuth connection.
Many workflows require paid Zoom plans, add-ons, or enabled account features.
Tool schemas may change, so the skill requires searching current Rube tools first.
Deletion and download workflows need explicit user approval because data may be sensitive.

use_cases.title: Schedule Team Meetings
use_cases.description: Create recurring or one-time meetings with waiting room, invitee, timezone, and recording settings.

use_cases.title: Review Attendance
use_cases.description: Collect past meeting participant lists and usage reports while respecting pagination and rate limits.

use_cases.title: Manage Webinar Registration
use_cases.description: List eligible webinars and add registrants through the connected Zoom account.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Zoom automation skill to create a 30 minute meeting for tomorrow at 10:00 AM in America/New_York. Use a waiting room and return the join link.

prompt_templates.title: List Upcoming Meetings
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Zoom automation skill to list my upcoming Zoom meetings for the next week. Follow pagination and summarize topic, start time, duration, and meeting ID.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve Attendance Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Zoom automation skill to get past participants for this meeting ID. Include join time, leave time, duration, and any pagination notes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Recordings Before Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Zoom automation skill to list cloud recordings for this date range. Do not delete anything. Identify recordings that may need retention review.

output_examples.input: Schedule a customer onboarding call for next Tuesday with a waiting room.
output_examples.output: A concise meeting summary with topic, scheduled time, duration, waiting room status, meeting ID, and participant join link.

output_examples.input: Show who attended yesterday's training session.
output_examples.output: A participant report with attendee names, emails when available, join and leave times, total duration, and pagination status.

output_examples.input: Find recordings from the first week of the month.
output_examples.output: A recording inventory with meeting titles, dates, file types, availability, and notes about download token requirements.

best_practices: Search Rube tools before each workflow so schemas match the current integration.
Confirm timezone, meeting type, and user intent before creating or updating meetings.
Ask for explicit confirmation before downloading recordings or deleting Zoom data.
anti_patterns: Do not assume free Zoom accounts support reports, webinars, recordings, or AI summaries.
Do not skip pagination when listing meetings, recordings, participants, or webinars.
Do not use permanent delete actions without a clear user request and confirmation.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to Zoom?
faq.answer: It uses Rube MCP and Composio Zoom tools, so a Rube MCP server and Zoom OAuth connection are required.

faq.question: Can it create Zoom meetings?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents the tool sequence and parameters for instant, scheduled, and recurring meetings.

faq.question: Can it manage recordings?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide listing, retrieving, and deleting cloud recordings when the Zoom account supports those features.

faq.question: Does it support webinars?
faq.answer: Yes, but webinar workflows require a paid Zoom plan with the webinar add-on enabled.

faq.question: Why must tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the agent use current names, parameters, and requirements.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive meetings?
faq.answer: Use it carefully. Recordings, participant reports, and tokens can contain sensitive data and should require user confirmation.
